content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
Low volatility environment with mixed breadth. Vol: 25.0%. Breadth: 3/8 sectors positive — Regime: RISK-ON.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Heavy Industry ABOVE TREND: EREGL (1W: +7.73% | 1M: +24.45%), KRDMD (1W: +6.53% | 1M: +24.66%), ASELS (1W: +5.99% | 1M: +31.23%). Construction & REIT ABOVE TREND: TKFEN (1W: +7.22% | 1M: +45.51%), ALARK (1W: +3.52% | 1M: +8.20%). Holding & Investment ABOVE TREND: DOHOL (1W: +7.57% | 1M: +13.87%).

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Transportation lagging: THYAO (1W: -4.71% | 1M: +4.76%) CORRECTION, PGSUS (1W: -5.05% | 1M: +2.91%) CORRECTION, TAVHL (1W: -9.65% | 1M: -9.50%) BELOW TREND. Banking under pressure: AKBNK (1W: -7.63% | 1M: +10.91%) CORRECTION, HALKB (1W: -6.24% | 1M: +7.74%) CORRECTION. Tech soft: KONTR (1W: -11.03% | 1M: +20.77%) CORRECTION.

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
EREGL (1W: +7.73% | 1M: +24.45%) ABOVE TREND, DOHOL (1W: +7.57% | 1M: +13.87%) ABOVE TREND, TKFEN (1W: +7.22% | 1M: +45.51%) ABOVE TREND, KRDMD (1W: +6.53% | 1M: +24.66%) ABOVE TREND, ASELS (1W: +5.99% | 1M: +31.23%) ABOVE TREND.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
TRALT (1W: -11.88% | 1M: -1.24%) BELOW TREND, TRMET (1W: -11.54% | 1M: -0.17%) BELOW TREND, KONTR (1W: -11.03% | 1M: +20.77%) CORRECTION, TAVHL (1W: -9.65% | 1M: -9.50%) BELOW TREND, AKBNK (1W: -7.63% | 1M: +10.91%) CORRECTION.

6. Momentum Stocks?
Highest 1W movers: TRALT (1W: -11.88% | 1M: -1.24%) BELOW TREND, TRMET (1W: -11.54% | 1M: -0.17%) BELOW TREND, KONTR (1W: -11.03% | 1M: +20.77%) CORRECTION, TAVHL (1W: -9.65% | 1M: -9.50%) BELOW TREND, EREGL (1W: +7.73% | 1M: +24.45%) ABOVE TREND.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banking in tactical pullback after recent gains; broad sector 1W: -4.84% with most names in CORRECTION — AKBNK (1W: -7.63% | 1M: +10.91%) CORRECTION, GARAN (1W: -4.02% | 1M: +10.05%) CORRECTION, ISCTR (1W: -3.04% | 1M: +13.53%) CORRECTION. Avoid adding exposure until 1W weakness stabilizes.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Industrials leading on cyclicals and defense; EREGL (1W: +7.73% | 1M: +24.45%) ABOVE TREND, KRDMD (1W: +6.53% | 1M: +24.66%) ABOVE TREND, ASELS (1W: +5.99% | 1M: +31.23%) ABOVE TREND, FROTO (1W: -4.20% | 1M: -3.07%) BELOW TREND is the main laggard. Bias overweight selective caps in this group.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Energy mixed: SASA (1W: +5.72% | 1M: +34.19%) ABOVE TREND, PETKM (1W: +3.91% | 1M: +12.63%) ABOVE TREND, TUPRS (1W: +3.04% | 1M: +4.94%) ABOVE TREND but TRALT (1W: -11.88% | 1M: -1.24%) BELOW TREND and TRMET (1W: -11.54% | 1M: -0.17%) BELOW TREND drag the group. Trade selectively around earnings and commodity moves.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Risk-on tactical stance: overweight Heavy Industry and Construction & REIT, hold selective Energy & Chemicals winners, underweight Banking, Transportation and weak Tech names. Keep liquidity to exploit corrections in strong cyclicals.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
TKFEN (1W: +7.22% | 1M: +45.51%) ABOVE TREND for momentum and catalysts, EREGL (1W: +7.73% | 1M: +24.45%) ABOVE TREND for cyclicality, ASELS (1W: +5.99% | 1M: +31.23%) ABOVE TREND for defense flows, AKBNK (1W: -7.63% | 1M: +10.91%) CORRECTION for banking volatility. Monitor TAVHL (1W: -9.65% | 1M: -9.50%) BELOW TREND for downside continuation.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Heavy Industry and Construction & REIT for upside leadership; Banking & Finance for risk-off flow if weakness continues. Energy & Chemicals as a swing sector — watch commodity price action.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Watch XU100 short-term: support at the 20-day MA (~6,000), secondary support near the 50-day MA (~5,700); resistance at recent highs (~6,400). Primary catalysts: CBRT communications and USD/TRY moves, global risk sentiment, commodity/energy price swings and key corporate earnings (TKFEN, TUPRS, ASELS).

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
RISK-ON reads with low volatility but mixed participation; leadership concentrated in Heavy Industry, Construction & REIT and select holdings. Opportunity: overweight cyclicals and construction names; risk: banking and transport corrections and macro/currency shocks that could flip sentiment quickly.
